questions.q: A stationary source is emitted sound at a fixed frequency <math><msub><mrow><mi>f</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math>, which is reflected by two cars approaching the source. The difference between the is frequencies of sound reflected from the car is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of <math><msub><mrow><mi>f</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math>. What is the difference in the speed of the cars (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)? The cars are moving at constant speeds much smaller than the speed of sound which is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>330</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>
questions.solution:
Firstly, car will be treated as an observer which is approaching the source. Then, it will be treated as a source, which is moving in the direction of sound.




Hence,

f1=fov+v1v-v1

f2=fov+v2v-v2

∴  f1-f2=1.2100fo

=fov+vv+v1-v+v2v-v2

Or

1.2100fo=2vv1-v2v-v1v-v2fo

as v1and v2 are very very less than v.

We can write, v-v1or v-v2≈v.

∴1.2100fo=2v1-v2vfo

Or  v1-v2=v×1.2200

=300×1.2200=1.98m s-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Sinusoidal waves 5 mm in amplitude are to be transmitted along a string having a linear mass density equal to $4 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}$. If the source can deliver an average power of 90 W and the string is under a tension of 100 N , then find the frequency (in Hz ) at which the source can operate. $\left(\right.$ take $\left.\pi^2=10\right):$
questions.solution:
$P=T\left(\frac{\partial y}{\partial t}\right)\left(\frac{\partial y}{\partial x}\right) ; y=A \sin (\omega t-k x+\phi)$
$\therefore\left|P_{\text {evg }}\right|=\frac{1}{2} T A^2 \omega K=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{T \mu} A^2 \omega^2=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{T \mu} A^2(2 \pi f)^2 \Rightarrow f=300 H z$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A train approaching a hill at a speed of $40 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ sounds a whistle of frequency 580 Hz . When it is at a distance of 1 km from a hill, a wind with a speed of $40 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ is blowing in the direction of motion of the train find
the frequency of the whistle as heard by an observer on the hill.
the distance from the hill at which the echo from the hill is heard by the driver and its frequency. (Velocity of sound in air $=1200 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$.)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
f^{\prime} & =\left(\frac{v+\omega}{v+\omega-v_s}\right) \mathrm{f} \\
& =\left(\frac{1200+40}{1200+40-40}\right) 580 \\
& \approx 599 \mathrm{~Hz}
\end{aligned}$

For echo to be heard by the driver, the source is to be considered at the hill having frequency 599 Hz .
$\mathrm{f}^{\prime}=\left(\frac{1200-40+40}{1200-40}\right) 599$
$\mathrm{t}_1($ wave to reach the hill $)=\frac{1}{1200+40}=\frac{1}{1240} \mathrm{hr} . \mathrm{s}$
in the above duration train moves $=\frac{40}{1240}=\frac{1}{31} \mathrm{~km}$.
Now the distance between train and hill $=1-\frac{1}{31}=\frac{30}{31} \mathrm{~km}$
After this instant echo will be heard
$40 \mathrm{t}+(1200-40) \mathrm{t}=30 / 31$
$\mathrm{t}=\frac{1}{1240} \mathrm{hrs}$

The distance travelled by the train in this time
$=40 \times \frac{1}{1240}=\frac{1}{31} \mathrm{~km}$

Distance from the hill $=1-\frac{2}{31}=0.935 \mathrm{~km}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A sound source of frequency $f_0=130 \mathrm{~Hz}$ is dropped from a height slightly greater than 250 m above the ground. At the same time a detector is thrown upwards with velocity $\mathrm{u}=50 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ along the same line. If the speed of sound is $v=300 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$, if the frequency (in Hz ) detected by the detector after $\mathrm{t}=5 \mathrm{~s}$ is $39 \times \mathrm{n}$, find the value of $\mathrm{n}\left(\right.$ Take $\left.\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.solution: After 5 s , speed of detector $=50-10 \times 5=0$ and that of source $=0+10 \times 5=50 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and the source has fallen a distance $=\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times(5)^2=125 \mathrm{~m}$ and the detector has rises a distance $=50 \times 5-\frac{1}{2} \times 10(5)^2=125 \mathrm{~m}$ So, $f^{\prime}=130\left(\frac{300-0}{300-50}\right)=156 \mathrm{~Hz}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A source of sound with natural frequency $F_0$ moves uniformly along a line separated from a stationary listener by a distance $\ell=42 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{~m}$. The velocity of source is equal to $\mathrm{n}=0.5$ fraction of velocity of sound. If the distance between source and listener at the moment when the listener receives actual frequency of the source is $(21 \times n)$ meter, find the value of $n$.
questions.solution: Let, $V_0 \rightarrow$ velocity of sound when source is $O$ at the velocity component of source towards listener is $V_{\mathrm{s}} \cos 90=0$ $\therefore \quad F^{\prime}=F_0\left(\frac{V_0}{V_0-V_S \cos 90}\right)=F_S$ time taken by sound to reach listener $=\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{V}_0}$. Distance moved by source in this time $=\left(\frac{\ell}{V_0}\right) \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{S}}$ $\therefore$ Distance between source and listener $=\sqrt{\ell^2+\mathrm{n}^2 \ell^2}=\ell \sqrt{1+\mathrm{n}^2}$ $=105 \mathrm{~m}$. 2. If the plank is displaced slightly by x towards left, then

questions.q: Two identical wires, one made of iron and the other of aluminum are stretched along - side on a sonometer board by equal stretching forces. [Density of iron $=7.5 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cc}$, density aluminum $=2.7$ $\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{cc}]$. The frequency of lowest harmonic for which both wires vibrate in unison, given that the length of the wires is 1 m , their diameters 1 mm and tension $75 \pi$ is given as $\beta \mathrm{kHz}$. Find the value of 12 $\beta$.
questions.solution:
(a) Given $\frac{P_{F_c}}{P_{A l}}=\frac{\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T}}{\rho_{\mathrm{Al}}}}}{\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T}}{\rho_{\mathrm{Fe}}}}}=\sqrt{\frac{7.5}{2.7}}=\frac{5}{3}$ Here fifth harmonic of $F_s=$ third harmonic of $\mathrm{A} l$ wire.
(b) Using $P_{F t}=5 ; f=\frac{5}{2 \times 1} \sqrt{\frac{75 \pi \times 4}{3.14 \times 10^{-6} \times 7.5 \times 10^3}}=500 \mathrm{~Hz}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A train moves towards a stationary observer with a speed of $34 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. The train sounds a whistle and its frequency registered by the observer is $f_1$. If the train's speed is reduced to $17 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$, the frequency registered is $f_2$. If the speed of sound is $340 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$, then the ratio $\frac{f_1}{f_2}$ is $\frac{10+x}{18}$. Calculate the value of $x$.
questions.solution:
$f_1=\left(\frac{340}{340-34}\right) f=\frac{10}{9} f$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { and } f_2=\left(\frac{340}{340-17}\right) f=\frac{20}{19} f \\ & \text { and } \frac{f_1}{f_2}=\frac{\frac{10}{9}}{\frac{20}{19}}=\frac{19}{18}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A tuning fork $A$ of frequency as given by the manufacturer is 512 Hz is being tested using an accurate oscillator. It is found that they produce 2 beats per second when the oscillator reads 514 Hz and 6 beats per second when it reads 510 Hz . The actual frequency of the fork in Hz is $(510+$ $x)$. Calculate the value of $x$.
questions.solution:
For $1^{\text {st }}$ reading of oscillator
$\begin{aligned} & f_A=(514 \pm 2) \mathrm{Hz} \\ \Rightarrow & f_{\mathrm{A}}=516 \mathrm{~Hz} \text { or } 512 \mathrm{~Hz}\end{aligned}$
For $2^{\text {nd }}$ reading of oscillator
$f_A=(510 \pm 6) H z$
$\Rightarrow f_A=516 \mathrm{~Hz}$ or 504 Hz
$\Rightarrow A$ has a frequency of 516 Hz
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two sound waves of frequencies 100 Hz and 102 Hz and having same amplitude ' A ' are interfering. A stationary detector, which can detect waves of amplitude greater than or equal to A , So, in a given time interval of 12 seconds, find the total duration in which detector is active.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & y_1=A \sin \omega_1 t \\ & y_2=A \sin \omega_2 t \\ & y_r=2 A \cos \left\{\frac{\left(\omega_2-\omega_1\right)}{2} t\right\}\left\{\sin \frac{\left(\omega_2+\omega_1\right)}{2} t\right\}\end{aligned}$ Resultant amplitude $\mathrm{A}_{\mathrm{r}}=2 \mathrm{~A}_0|\cos (\Delta \omega) \mathrm{t} / 2|$ $\begin{aligned} & (\Delta \omega) \frac{t}{2}=\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow t=\frac{1}{4} s \\ & (\Delta \omega) \frac{t}{2}=\frac{\pi}{3} \Rightarrow t=\frac{1}{6} s\end{aligned}$ In one cycle of intensity of $1 / 2 \mathrm{~s}$, the detector remain idle for $2\left(\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{6}\right) s=\frac{1}{6} \sec$ $\therefore$ In $1 / 2$ sec cycle, active time is $\left(\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{6}\right)=1 / 3 \mathrm{sec}$ $\therefore$ In 12 sec interval, active time is $12 \times \frac{(1 / 3)}{(1 / 2)}=8 \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A source of sound of frequency 600 Hz is placed inside water. The speed of sound in water is $1500 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and in air it is $300 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The frequency of sound recorded by an observer who is standing in air is ___ Hz
questions.solution: Frequency does not change upon refraction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: At a particular instant a source of sound of frequency 100 Hz is at $(2 \mathrm{~m}, 1 \mathrm{~m})$ and an observer is at $(5 \mathrm{~m}, 5 \mathrm{~m})$. The velocity of source and observer are $15(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}) \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $5 \hat{i}+15 \hat{j} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ respectively at this instant .The velocity of sound in air is $330 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Find the frequency of sound (in Hz ) received by the observer.
questions.solution: $\overrightarrow{S O}=4 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}$ Component of velocity along SO $\begin{aligned} & v_s=15(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}) \cdot\left(\frac{3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}}{5}\right)=30 \\ & v_0=(5 \hat{i}+15 \hat{j}) \cdot\left(\frac{3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}}{5}\right)=15 \\ & f=\frac{330-15}{330-30} \times 100=105 \mathrm{~Hz} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A transverse sinusoidal wave moves along a string in the positive $x$-direction at a speed of $10 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$. At a particular time $t$, the snap-shot of the wave is shown in figure. The speed of point $P$ is $x \sqrt{2} \pi \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ when its displacement is $\frac{10}{\sqrt{2}} \mathrm{~cm}$, then the value of $x$ is
(wavelength of the above wave $\lambda=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m}$ )
questions.solution:
$y=10 \cos 4 \pi x \quad \frac{10}{\sqrt{2}}=10 \cos 4 \pi x$
$\frac{\partial y}{\partial x}=-40 \pi \sin 4 \pi x \quad \frac{\partial y}{\partial x}=-\frac{40 \pi}{\sqrt{2}} \quad V_{\text {particle }}=-V_{\text {wave }} \frac{\partial y}{\partial x}$
$\mathrm{V}_{\text {particle }}=10 \times\left(\frac{40 \pi}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=\frac{400 \pi}{\sqrt{2}} \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}=2 \sqrt{2} \pi \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
